title: PM Modi Addresses Seychelles Parliament, Calls Indian Ocean an 'Ocean of Opportunity'
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed the Seychelles Parliament during his state visit, declaring India's vision to make the Indian Ocean an 'Ocean of Opportunity'. He stated that the Indian Ocean 'connects us, not separates us', emphasising India's maritime partnerships with island nations. Modi also highlighted the disproportionate impact of climate change on small island developing states. The visit is part of India's broader Indian Ocean Region (IOR) outreach under its SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) doctrine.
exam_angle: Connects to India's SAGAR doctrine, Neighbourhood First policy extended to Indian Ocean island states, and India's strategic competition with China in the IOR. Seychelles is a key partner under India's maritime security architecture.
key_facts: PM Modi addressed Seychelles National Assembly — a rare honour for a foreign dignitary
Vision: Indian Ocean as 'Ocean of Opportunity'
Relevant doctrine: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region)
Climate vulnerability of small island states flagged as a key concern

title: India Rules Out Indus Waters Treaty Relief for Pakistan, Rejects Track-II Reports
summary: India has officially ruled out providing any relief to Pakistan under the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) and rejected reports of Track-II diplomatic engagements suggesting a thaw on water-sharing arrangements. The government's position signals continued hardening of stance following the suspension of the IWT in the aftermath of the Pahalgam terror attack in April 2026. India maintains that cross-border terrorism and bilateral talks cannot go hand in hand. Pakistan remains heavily dependent on the Indus river system for agriculture and hydropower.
exam_angle: The Indus Waters Treaty (1960, brokered by World Bank) is a perennial UPSC topic; its suspension and India's leverage over upstream water resources are critical issues in India-Pakistan relations under GS Paper 2 IR and GS Paper 3 (water security).
key_facts: Indus Waters Treaty signed in 1960, brokered by the World Bank
India suspended the treaty following the April 2026 Pahalgam terror attack
India controls upper riparian rights over the Indus river system
Track-II diplomacy reports of negotiations rejected by MEA

title: India Rejects Pakistan's Allegations Linking New Delhi to Karachi Terror Attack
summary: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) formally rejected Pakistan's allegations attempting to link India to a terrorist attack on a Rangers base in Karachi that killed four soldiers and resulted in six terrorists being neutralised. MEA called the allegations 'baseless and preposterous'. Pakistan's Balochistan Liberation Army (BLA) has been active in Karachi, and Islamabad has a pattern of attributing internal security failures to India. The denial underscores ongoing diplomatic tensions between the two nuclear-armed neighbours.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of India-Pakistan bilateral tensions, the MEA's role in diplomatic communication, and Pakistan's internal security challenges (Baloch insurgency). Relevant for GS Paper 2 IR and Essay on India's security environment.
key_facts: Attack target: Pakistan Rangers base in Karachi
Toll: 4 soldiers dead, 6 terrorists killed, 1 captured
India's response: MEA called allegations 'baseless'
Suspected perpetrator: Balochistan Liberation Army (BLA)

title: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Set to Enter New Phase from July 2026
summary: July 2026 marks a significant milestone in India-UK trade relations as provisions of their bilateral Free Trade Agreement (FTA) begin to take effect, described as 'the biggest chapter in India-UK trade relations'. The FTA, concluded after over three years of negotiations, covers goods, services, investments, and intellectual property. It is expected to nearly double bilateral trade to USD 120 billion by 2030. Key sectors benefiting include Indian textiles, pharmaceuticals, and IT services, while UK gains easier access for Scotch whisky, automobiles, and financial services.
exam_angle: India-UK FTA is a landmark trade agreement and a high-priority UPSC topic under GS Paper 3 (Economy/Trade) and GS Paper 2 (IR). Key aspects include trade liberalisation, tariff reduction schedules, and impact on domestic industries.
key_facts: India-UK FTA implementation begins: July 2026
Target: Nearly double bilateral trade to USD 120 billion by 2030
India gains: Better market access for textiles, pharma, IT services
UK gains: Reduced tariffs on Scotch whisky, automobiles, financial services

title: FATF Warns: Global Terror Financing Going Digital in Latest Report
summary: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) has released a report warning that global terror financing is increasingly shifting to digital channels, including cryptocurrencies, mobile payment platforms, and decentralised finance (DeFi) systems. FATF flagged that traditional financial monitoring mechanisms are struggling to track these digital flows. The report calls for enhanced regulatory frameworks and international cooperation to address emerging risks. India, as a FATF member, will need to align its counter-terror financing regulations accordingly.
exam_angle: FATF is a critical institution in UPSC current affairs — its grey list and black list directly impact countries' financial access. Digital terror financing connects GS Paper 3 (Internal Security, Cybersecurity) and GS Paper 2 (International organisations).
key_facts: FATF: Financial Action Task Force — inter-governmental body combating money laundering and terror financing
Key finding: Terror financing migrating to cryptocurrencies and DeFi platforms
India is an active FATF member since 2010
FATF has two lists: 'Grey list' (increased monitoring) and 'Black list' (non-cooperative jurisdictions)

title: France's Worst Heatwave Claims Over 4,000 Lives; Europe Battles Record Temperatures
summary: France recorded over 4,000 deaths attributable to what authorities describe as its worst-ever heatwave, with an additional 1,000 deaths reported in the latest tally. Extreme heat has swept across Europe, with several countries recording all-time temperature highs. Scientists link the increasing frequency and intensity of such heatwaves to anthropogenic climate change and the urban heat island effect. The crisis has renewed calls for stronger climate adaptation measures and accelerated emissions reduction commitments under the Paris Agreement framework.
exam_angle: Heatwave mortality connects to GS Paper 3 (Environment & Disaster Management) and GS Paper 1 (Geography — climate phenomena). Links to Paris Agreement obligations, urban heat islands, and climate justice discussions.
key_facts: France heatwave death toll: Over 4,000
Described as France's worst-ever heatwave
Cause: Attributed to climate change amplifying heat extremes
Policy relevance: Calls for enhanced NDCs under the Paris Agreement

title: PM Modi Hails C-295 Aircraft in Mann Ki Baat 135th Episode, Highlights Defence Self-Reliance
summary: In the 135th episode of Mann Ki Baat, Prime Minister Modi highlighted India's progress in defence manufacturing self-reliance, specifically citing the C-295 military transport aircraft. The C-295 is being manufactured in India through a partnership between Tata Advanced Systems and Airbus Defence, marking India's first private-sector military aircraft manufacturing facility. Modi stated: 'From seas to skies, India is becoming self-reliant', underscoring the Atmanirbhar Bharat push in defence. The Vadodara facility is expected to produce 40 aircraft for the Indian Air Force.
exam_angle: C-295 aircraft project is a flagship example of Make in India in defence and private-sector participation in defence manufacturing. Links to GS Paper 3 (Defence/Economy) and Mann Ki Baat as a government communication platform.
key_facts: C-295 manufactured by: Tata Advanced Systems + Airbus Defence partnership
Location: Vadodara, Gujarat — India's first private military aircraft manufacturing facility
IAF order: 40 C-295 transport aircraft
Highlighted in: Mann Ki Baat Episode 135

title: Sonam Wangchuk Joins Hunger Strike at Jantar Mantar Over Ladakh Statehood Demand
summary: Climate activist and engineer Sonam Wangchuk joined a hunger strike at Jantar Mantar, New Delhi, along with members of the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) protest, demanding statehood and Sixth Schedule protections for Ladakh. Six students also participated in the fast. Wangchuk has been at the forefront of demands for constitutional safeguards for Ladakh's land, employment, and cultural rights since it became a Union Territory in 2019. The Sixth Schedule of the Constitution provides for autonomous district councils in tribal areas of the Northeast.
exam_angle: Ladakh's constitutional status (UT without legislature), the Sixth Schedule provisions, and the demand for statehood are high-value UPSC Polity topics. Connects to Articles 3, 244, and the reorganisation of J&K under the J&K Reorganisation Act, 2019.
key_facts: Ladakh became a Union Territory (without legislature) on October 31, 2019 under J&K Reorganisation Act
Key demands: Statehood for Ladakh + Sixth Schedule constitutional protections
Sixth Schedule: Provides for Autonomous District Councils in tribal areas (Articles 244 and 275)
Protest venue: Jantar Mantar, New Delhi

title: Ram Temple Embezzlement Case: Legal Complexity Around Juristic Person and Minor's Assets
summary: The Ayodhya Ram Temple embezzlement case has raised significant legal questions as it concerns the assets of a 'juristic person' — the deity Ram Lalla Virajman — and a minor. The Allahabad High Court and legal experts are examining how theft or misappropriation of temple assets owned by a juristic person under Hindu law is to be treated under criminal law. Lawyers in Ayodhya have opposed attempts to dilute the case. This case brings the concept of a deity as a legal entity — a well-established principle in Indian jurisprudence — into focus.
exam_angle: The concept of a 'juristic person' (legal person) in Indian law, including the recognition of deities as legal entities capable of holding property, is an important polity/legal concept for UPSC. Ram Lalla was recognised as a juristic person in the Ayodhya verdict (2019).
key_facts: Ram Lalla Virajman recognised as a 'juristic person' in Supreme Court's Ayodhya verdict (November 2019)
Case involves alleged embezzlement of assets belonging to the Ram Temple Trust
Legal question: Criminal liability for theft of property owned by a juristic person
Ayodhya lawyers have opposed discharge of accused

title: India's Green Hydrogen from Nuclear Reactor: A Step Towards Clean Energy Transition
summary: India has taken a significant step towards clean fuel production by developing a process to generate green hydrogen using nuclear reactor energy. This approach uses nuclear-powered electrolysis to split water into hydrogen and oxygen without carbon emissions, potentially offering a large-scale, reliable, and low-carbon hydrogen production pathway. The development aligns with India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, which targets production of 5 million metric tonnes of green hydrogen per year by 2030. Nuclear-sourced hydrogen could reduce dependence on intermittent renewable sources for electrolysis.
exam_angle: Green Hydrogen is a top UPSC priority topic under GS Paper 3 (Science & Technology, Energy). Links to India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, energy transition goals, and nuclear energy's expanding civilian role.
key_facts: National Green Hydrogen Mission target: 5 MMT/year green hydrogen production by 2030
Method: Nuclear-powered electrolysis (water splitting) — zero carbon emissions
Advantage over solar/wind hydrogen: Reliable baseload power for continuous production
India's overall green hydrogen investment target: ₹8 lakh crore by 2030

title: India's First AICTE-Approved BTech in AI with Indian Knowledge Systems Focus Launched
summary: India has approved its first AICTE-sanctioned Bachelor of Technology (BTech) programme in Artificial Intelligence that integrates Indian Knowledge Systems (IKS) into the curriculum. The programme aims to blend modern AI and machine learning techniques with traditional Indian epistemology, mathematics, and scientific heritage. AICTE (All India Council for Technical Education) has been promoting IKS integration across technical education as part of the National Education Policy 2020 framework. The initiative seeks to develop AI professionals rooted in Indian intellectual traditions.
exam_angle: AICTE's regulatory role in technical education, NEP 2020's emphasis on Indian Knowledge Systems, and AI education policy are key current affairs for GS Paper 2 (Governance/Education) and GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech).
key_facts: First AICTE-approved BTech in AI with Indian Knowledge Systems integration
Regulatory body: AICTE (All India Council for Technical Education)
Policy framework: National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 promotes IKS in higher education
Objective: Blend AI/ML with traditional Indian scientific and philosophical traditions

title: West Bengal Assembly to Table Bill on Preventive Detention to Check Criminal Gangs
summary: The West Bengal state government is set to introduce a bill in the state assembly that would allow for preventive detention of persons suspected of 'thuggery' and criminal gang activities. The proposed legislation would permit authorities to detain individuals without trial for a specified period as a preventive measure. Preventive detention laws in India are governed by Article 22 of the Constitution, which provides both the power and the safeguards against arbitrary detention. The bill comes amid rising political violence in the state.
exam_angle: Preventive detention is a foundational Polity topic — Article 22 (rights against arbitrary detention), the 44th Amendment limits on preventive detention, and state-level preventive detention laws are standard UPSC questions.
key_facts: Article 22 of the Constitution governs preventive detention in India
Maximum period of preventive detention without Advisory Board review: 3 months (extended to 12 weeks by 44th Amendment)
State's right to legislate on public order: Entry 1, State List, Seventh Schedule
Context: Rising political violence in West Bengal

title: National Traders' Welfare Board Endorses Wider ONDC Adoption Among Small Traders
summary: The National Traders' Welfare Board has formally backed the wider adoption of the Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC) among small and medium traders across India. ONDC is a government-backed initiative by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) designed to democratise e-commerce by enabling interoperability across platforms, unlike proprietary systems like Amazon or Flipkart. The board's endorsement signals efforts to bring unorganised retail into the formal digital economy, potentially benefiting millions of small traders.
exam_angle: ONDC is a high-priority GS Paper 3 topic connecting digital economy, inclusion of small businesses, and government's e-commerce policy. The National Traders' Welfare Board is under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry.
key_facts: ONDC: Open Network for Digital Commerce — launched by DPIIT, Ministry of Commerce
Key feature: Open protocol allowing interoperability across e-commerce platforms
Objective: Democratise digital commerce for small/micro traders
Endorsed by: National Traders' Welfare Board

title: India's Frozen Shrimp Exports: China Emerges as Key Growth Market, US Remains Top Destination in FY26
summary: Data for FY2025-26 shows that China has emerged as the fastest-growing market for India's frozen shrimp exports, even as the United States continues to be the largest destination by value. India is the world's largest shrimp exporter, with the marine products sector being a significant contributor to agricultural exports. The rise of China as a key buyer diversifies India's export risk and reflects growing Chinese demand for seafood amid its own aquaculture challenges. The Marine Products Export Development Authority (MPEDA) oversees this sector.
exam_angle: India's marine product exports and MPEDA's role are relevant for GS Paper 3 (Agriculture/Economy). India-China trade dynamics and export diversification strategies are important IR-Economy interface topics.
key_facts: India: World's largest frozen shrimp exporter
FY26 top market by value: United States
FY26 fastest-growing market: China
Regulatory body: MPEDA (Marine Products Export Development Authority) under Ministry of Commerce

title: Aurangzeb's Akhbarat: Mughal Empire's Intelligence and News Reporting System Examined
summary: A detailed historical analysis by the BBC examines the 'akhbarat' — the sophisticated news-reporting and intelligence system of the Mughal Empire under Emperor Aurangzeb. Akhbarat were official newsletters compiled by 'waqia-nawis' (news-writers) stationed across the empire who reported daily events to the Mughal court. These documents reveal the administrative functioning, court politics, provincial affairs, and even market prices of Mughal India. Historians consider the akhbarat one of the most detailed bureaucratic records of pre-colonial India.
exam_angle: Mughal administrative history, including intelligence and communication systems, is a GS Paper 1 (History/Culture) topic. Akhbarat as a source of historical evidence and the Mughal bureaucratic apparatus are relevant for history optional and general studies.
key_facts: Akhbarat: Official Mughal newsletters/news reports — a primary historical source
Waqia-nawis: Imperial news-writers stationed across the Mughal Empire
Emperor Aurangzeb (r. 1658–1707): Last of the major Mughal emperors
Historical significance: Reveals Mughal administration, economy, and court politics in detail

title: Pakistan Suspends Geo News Licence for 15 Days Over Muharram Programme Content
summary: Pakistan's media regulatory authority suspended the broadcast licence of Geo News — Pakistan's largest private news channel — for 15 days following objections to content aired during a Muharram religious programme. The suspension highlights the tense relationship between Pakistani authorities and independent media, a recurring issue in that country. Geo News has previously faced pressure from the military establishment over its editorial positions. Media freedom in Pakistan has been consistently ranked poorly by international press freedom indices.
exam_angle: Press freedom, media regulation, and the state of democracy in Pakistan are relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) and comparative governance. India's contrast with Pakistan on press freedom indices could be an essay angle.
key_facts: Geo News: Pakistan's largest private TV news channel
Suspension period: 15 days
Reason: Objectionable content aired during Muharram programme
Pakistan's press freedom: Ranked 158/180 in RSF Press Freedom Index 2024

title: Sarvesh Kushare Breaks Indian High Jump National Record
summary: Indian high jumper Sarvesh Kushare has broken the national record in the high jump event, setting a new benchmark and targeting the 2.35 metre mark next. Kushare's achievement marks a significant milestone in Indian athletics, a discipline where India has historically struggled to compete at the global level. The jump improves India's prospects heading into international competitions including the World Athletics Championships and the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics qualification cycle. The Athletics Federation of India (AFI) governs track and field sports in India.
exam_angle: Persons in news from sports with national record achievements are standard current affairs for UPSC. Indian athletics performance, bodies like AFI, and Olympic qualification norms are relevant for GS Paper 2 (Governance/Sports Policy).
key_facts: Athlete: Sarvesh Kushare — Indian high jumper
Achievement: New Indian national record in high jump
Next target: 2.35 metre mark
Governing body: Athletics Federation of India (AFI)

title: India's IT Sector Faces Continued Headwinds from GenAI and Geopolitical Uncertainty: JPMorgan
summary: Investment bank JPMorgan has noted that India's Information Technology sector is likely to face sustained growth pressure due to two converging forces: the disruption caused by Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) automating traditional IT services, and ongoing geopolitical uncertainties affecting client spending in key markets like the United States and Europe. India's IT sector — dominated by TCS, Infosys, Wipro, and HCL Technologies — contributes approximately 7.5% of GDP and employs over 5 million professionals. The report signals a structural transition period for the industry.
exam_angle: India's IT sector's economic contribution, employment, and vulnerability to AI disruption are GS Paper 3 topics. The impact of GenAI on services exports and India's future in the global digital economy are important for essays and economy papers.
key_facts: India's IT sector contribution: ~7.5% of GDP; employs 5+ million professionals
Key risk 1: GenAI automating traditional IT/BPO tasks
Key risk 2: Geopolitical uncertainty suppressing client IT spending in the US and Europe
Source of assessment: JPMorgan research report
